title: Documenting Client Suggestions
content:
Why Client Suggestions Matter


Client suggestions are one of the most valuable sources of product feedback. When a user takes the time to request a feature or improvement, they're telling you exactly what would make the platform more useful to them — and likely to many others. Every suggestion, no matter how small, should be documented, acknowledged, and forwarded to the product team.


Important: A suggestion is not a support ticket. It doesn't need a resolution — it needs recognition and proper routing. The client should leave the conversation feeling heard, not dismissed.



How to Receive a Suggestion


When a client shares an idea for a feature or improvement, follow these steps:


  1. Listen fully without interrupting. Let them explain the idea in their own words before responding.
  2. Ask clarifying questions if needed. What problem would this solve for them? How do they currently work around it?
  3. Acknowledge the value of their input. Even if the suggestion isn't feasible, the fact that they shared it is worth appreciating.
  4. Document it immediately. Use the Ticketing system with the Client Suggestion category so it's properly tracked.


Logging the Suggestion — Ticketing Procedure


  1. Navigate to Ticketing in the sidebar.
  2. Click New Ticket.
  3. Set Category to Client Suggestion.
  4. Write a clear Subject summarizing the suggestion (e.g., "Dark mode option for student dashboard").
  5. In the Agent Action field, document:
    • What the client suggested (in their own words if possible)
    • The problem it would solve for them
    • Any current workaround they're using
    • The client's name and email for follow-up
  6. Click Submit Ticket.

This creates a permanent record the product team can review. It also shows the client that their feedback is taken seriously enough to be formally documented.



Acknowledging the Client — What to Say


After documenting, thank the client sincerely. Avoid making promises about implementation — you don't know if or when it will be built. Instead, focus on appreciation:


  • "That's a really thoughtful suggestion — I can see how that would make things easier for you."
  • "Thank you for sharing this. I've documented it and sent it to our product team for review."
  • "We take client feedback seriously. Ideas like yours help us improve the platform for everyone."
  • "While I can't promise a timeline, your suggestion is now in our system and will be reviewed."


What Not to Say


  • "We'll add that soon." — Never make promises you can't keep.
  • "That's not something we're working on." — Dismissive and discouraging.
  • "You're the first person to ask for that." — Makes them feel like an outlier rather than a contributor.
  • "That would be too hard to build." — Don't discuss technical limitations with clients.


Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "I really appreciate you taking the time to share that idea — feedback like this helps us build a better platform."
  • "I've logged your suggestion so our product team can review it. Is there anything else you'd like me to note?"
  • "If we have any follow-up questions about your suggestion, we may reach out — is that okay?"
  • "Thank you again. Suggestions from users like you are how we decide what to build next."

Always end on a positive note. A client who feels heard is more likely to stay loyal — even if their specific feature never gets built.
